hooks.command:
bash -c 'COMMAND=$(echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r .command 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "git commit.*-m.*"O[0-9]+:"; then OPT=$(echo "$COMMAND" | grep -oE "O[0-9]+" | head -1); echo "
🧪 [HOOK] Optimization $OPT committed. Test should run next.
   Run: python3 scripts/run-test-suite.py --prepare --label $OPT
   Then Claude launches 20 parallel agents.
   Then: python3 scripts/run-test-suite.py --consolidate --label $OPT
   Then: python3 scripts/run-test-suite.py --compare --from <previous> --to $OPT"; fi'

_documentation.purpose: Triggers test workflow notification after optimization commits
_documentation.flow: Hook detects 'git commit -m "OX:..."' and reminds Claude to run test suite
test_command_sequence: 2. Claude launches 20 Agent tool calls in parallel (one per Q1-Q20)
3. Each Agent reads its prompt from $VAULT_PATH/meta/tests/prompts/OX/Q*.md
7. If REVERT: git revert HEAD; if IMPLEMENT: continue to next optimization
